q:

They are placed on a horizontal table parallel to each other with their N poles pointing towards the south. They have a common magnetic equator and are separated by a distance of 20.0 cm. The value of the resultant horizontal magnetic induction at the mid-point O of the line joining their centres is close to
( Horizontal component of earth's magnetic induction is 3.6×10-5  Wb m-2 )

<math><msub><mrow><mi>V</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>40</mn><mi> </mi><mi>m</mi><mi>V</mi><mo>;</mo><msub><mrow><mi>V</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>W</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>135</mn><mi> </mi><mi>m</mi><mi>V</mi></math>  with right side of pilot at high voltage

<math><msub><mrow><mi>V</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>45</mn><mi> </mi><mi>m</mi><mi>V</mi><mo>;</mo><msub><mrow><mi>V</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>W</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>120</mn><mi> </mi><mi>m</mi><mi>V</mi></math> with left side of pilot at higher voltage
solution:
V B = B H 5 2 4 0

B H = B cos θ, ....

Bv=Bsinθ

B H = 5 5 × 1 0 - 5 3

B v = 1 0 3 × 1 0 - 5 T

V B = 5 5 3 × 1 0 - 5 × 5 × 2 4 0

V B = 44.6 mV = 4 5 mV

V w = B v ℓ V  = 10 3 × 10 −5 ×15×240

= 1 0 - 4 × 1 2 0 0

V w = 1 2 0 mV

(left side at fighter voltage)

subject: Physics
q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason(R).
Assertion (A) : Magnetic monopoles do not exist.
Reason (R) : Magnetic field lines are continuous and form closed loops.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason(R).
Assertion (A) : Magnetic monopoles do not exist.
Reason (R) : Magnetic field lines are continuous and form closed loops.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both $(\mathrm{A})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$ are correct but $(\mathrm{R})$ is not the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but $(\mathrm{R})$ is not correct
Both $(\mathrm{A})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$ are correct and $(\mathrm{R})$ is the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is not correct but ( $\mathrm{R}$) is correct
solution: Both statements are correct and reason is also the correct explanation of assertion.

subject: Physics
q:
A solenoid has a core made of material with relative permeability $400$. The magnetic field produced in the interior of solenoid is $1.0$ T. The magnetic intensity in SI units is $\alpha \times 10^5$. The value of $\alpha$ is ______.
(Free space permeability $\mu_0=4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ SI units.)
question:
A solenoid has a core made of material with relative permeability $400$. The magnetic field produced in the interior of solenoid is $1.0$ T. The magnetic intensity in SI units is $\alpha \times 10^5$. The value of $\alpha$ is ______.
(Free space permeability $\mu_0=4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ SI units.)
solution:
The magnetic field $B$ inside the solenoid is related to the magnetic intensity $H$ by the formula:

$B = \mu H = \mu_0 \mu_r H$

Rearranging for $H$, we get:

$H = \dfrac{B}{\mu_0 \mu_r}$

Substituting the given values ($B = 1.0$ T, $\mu_r = 400$, and $\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ T m/A):

$H = \dfrac{1.0}{4\pi \times 10^{-7} \times 400}$

$H = \dfrac{1.0}{1600\pi \times 10^{-7}}$

$H = \dfrac{1}{16\pi \times 10^{-5}}$

$H = \dfrac{1}{16\pi} \times 10^5$ A/m

Comparing this with the given expression $H = \alpha \times 10^5$, we find:

$\alpha = \dfrac{1}{16\pi}$

Answer: $\dfrac{1}{16\pi}$

q:

Make the correspondence of these bars with their material being diamagnetic ( $\mathrm{D})$, ferromagnetic (F) and paramagnetic $(\mathrm{P})$ :
question:
Three identical bars A, B and C are made of different magnetic materials. When kept in a uniform magnetic field, the field lines around them look as follows:


Make the correspondence of these bars with their material being diamagnetic ( $\mathrm{D})$, ferromagnetic (F) and paramagnetic $(\mathrm{P})$ :
solution:
Diamagnetic materials are repelled in an external magnetic field.
Bar $B$ represents diamagnetic materials.

subject: Physics
q: A perfectly diamagnetic sphere has a small spherical cavity at its centre, which is filled with a paramagnetic substance. The whole system is placed in a uniform magnetic field B→. Then the field inside the paramagnetic substance is:
question: A perfectly diamagnetic sphere has a small spherical cavity at its centre, which is filled with a paramagnetic substance. The whole system is placed in a uniform magnetic field B→. Then the field inside the paramagnetic substance is:
options: much large than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> and parallel to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>

much large than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> but opposite to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></math>
solution: When magnetic field is applied diamagnetic substance produces magnetic field in opposite direction so net magnetic field will be zero.
